message:
🌱 dify: Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated Execution and (#1886)

* 🌱 Add dify: Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated mission

* 🌱 Add dify: Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace closed dify PR #13058 with merged PR #14863

The original fix PR langgenius/dify#13058 was closed without merging.
Replace with langgenius/dify#14863 which was merged and implements
the loop node feature.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+{

+  "name": "dify-12354-enhancing-workflow-with-loop-and-sleep-nodes-for-repeated-execution-a",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "dify: Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated Execution and Interval Control",
+    "description": "Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated Execution and Interval Control. Requested by 12+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current dify deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your dify version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify\nhelm list -n dify 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working dify installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review dify configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant dify configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify\nkubectl get configmap -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dify\n```\n### Self Checks\n\n- [X] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/langgenius/dify/issues), including closed ones."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated",
+        "description": "# Summary\n\nThis PR introduces the Loop Node, a feature designed to enable workflows or processes to execute repeatedly until specific conditions or formats are met. The node allows users to define internal variables and outputs that determine whether to exit the loop. A maximum iteration limit can also be set to prevent infinite loops. \n\nThe addition of the Loop Node addresses the community’s and team’s demand for executing repeated HTTP interface calls or invoking LLMs multiple times in a\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify\nkubectl get events -n dify --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "# Summary\n\nThis PR introduces the Loop Node, a feature designed to enable workflows or processes to execute repeatedly until specific conditions or formats are met. The node allows users to define internal variables and outputs that determine whether to exit the loop. A maximum iteration limit can also be set to prevent infinite loops.",

+    }
